# Services Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign

## User Story

**As a** prospective client browsing the landing page,  
**I want to** see a clearly presented grid of services with outcome-focused descriptions,  
**so that** I can quickly identify which offerings are relevant to my needs and feel confident in the practice's capabilities.

---

## Description

This story delivers the Services section: six cards, each describing a service with outcome-oriented copy (what the client gains, not just what is done), an icon, and a hover interaction. Cards are read from `services.json` to keep content centralised and prevent messaging drift.

**Business value:** Clearly communicated services reduce sales friction and support self-qualification by visitors before they reach out.

---

## Scenario

### Scenario 1: Visitor scrolls to the services section

**Given** a visitor scrolls past the hero or clicks the secondary hero CTA (`#services`)  
**When** the services section enters the viewport  
**Then** a section heading is displayed  
**And** exactly 6 service cards are rendered in a responsive grid  
**And** each card shows: an icon, a service name, and a short outcome-focused description

### Scenario 2: Visitor hovers over a service card (pointer device)

**Given** the visitor is on a device with a pointer (mouse/trackpad)  
**When** they move the cursor over a service card  
**Then** the card lifts visually (box-shadow or transform elevation change)  
**And** the card border displays the accent colour  
**And** the card icon tints to the accent colour  
**And** the transition is smooth, using the motion timing token

### Scenario 3: Visitor views services on a touch device

**Given** the visitor is on a mobile or tablet device without a hover-capable pointer  
**When** the services section is visible  
**Then** cards are displayed in their default (non-hovered) state  
**And** no hover styles persist after tap  
**And** all card content remains readable without any interaction

### Scenario 4: services.json content is updated

**Given** the content team updates a service name or description in `services.json`  
**When** the page is rebuilt/reloaded  
**Then** the updated service copy appears on the corresponding card without any code changes to the component

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### Core Functionality
- [ ] Exactly 6 service cards are rendered, sourced from `services.json`
- [ ] Each card displays: icon, service name, and an outcome-focused description
- [ ] Section includes a heading identifying it as the services section
- [ ] Section is reachable via the `#services` anchor link from the hero

### Interaction & Hover
- [ ] Hover state applies on pointer devices only (use `@media (hover: hover)`)
- [ ] Hover effect includes: card elevation lift, accent border, icon colour tint
- [ ] Transitions use the shared motion timing CSS token
- [ ] No hover styles are stuck/visible in the default state on touch-only devices

### Content Consistency
- [ ] Service names and descriptions are consumed from `services.json`; no hardcoded copy in the component
- [ ] Updates to `services.json` are reflected without component code changes

### Responsive Layout
- [ ] On desktop (≥1024px): 3-column grid
- [ ] On tablet (768px–1023px): 2-column grid
- [ ] On mobile (<768px): single-column stack; no horizontal overflow
- [ ] Card height is consistent within a row (equal-height grid cells)

### Accessibility
- [ ] Cards use semantic markup (e.g. `<article>` or `<li>`) with meaningful headings
- [ ] Icons are decorative and hidden from screen readers (`aria-hidden="true"`)
- [ ] Focus-visible styles are applied for keyboard navigation through cards
- [ ] Section heading level is semantically correct relative to the page `<h1>`

---

## Prerequisites

- [Hero Section — Modern Landing Page Redesign](./hero-section-landing-page.md) — design token CSS variables must be declared before this section can use them
